s-1.ptxt: INSTALL BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install a new O-ring, the brake master cylinder sub-assembly and the front brake tube way with the 2 nuts .
Connect the connector.
Install the brake master cylinder to way tube and temporarily tighten the 6 brake lines.
Using SST, fully tighten the 6 brake lines.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to a torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made without VSC)

s2.ptxt: Install a new O-ring, the brake master cylinder sub-assembly and the front brake tube way with the 2 nuts .
Connect the connector.
Install the brake master cylinder to way tube and temporarily tighten the 6 brake lines.
Using SST, fully tighten the 6 brake lines.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to a torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made with VSC)

s2.ptxt: Install a new O-ring, the brake master cylinder sub-assembly and the front brake tube way with the 2 nuts .
Connect the connector.
Install the brake master cylinder to way tube and temporarily tighten the 6 brake lines.
Using SST, fully tighten the 6 brake lines.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to a torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: CONNECT CLUTCH RESERVOIR TUBE (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Connect the clutch reservoir tube to the brake master cylinder sub-assembly and slide the clip.

s-1.ptxt: FILL RESERVOIR WITH BRAKE FLUID

s2.ptxt: Fill the reservoir with brake fluid.

specitem.ptxt: SAE J1703 or FMVSS No. 116 DOT3

s-1.ptxt: BLEED CLUTCH PIPE LINE (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Fill the brake reservoir with brake fluid and bleed the clutch system.

s-1.ptxt: BLEED MASTER CYLINDER

item.ptxt: If the master cylinder is reinstalled or if the reservoir becomes empty, bleed the air from the master cylinder.
To avoid brake fluid from adhering, cover the painted surface with a shop rag or piece of cloth.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, disconnect the 2 brake lines from the master cylinder.
Slowly depress the brake pedal and hold it (*1).
Cover the 2 outer holes with fingers, and release the brake pedal (*2).
Repeat (*1) and (*2) 3 or 4 times.
Using SST, connect the brake lines to the master cylinder.

torque.subtitle: without SST
with SST
for TMMK made with VSC without SST
for TMMK made with VSC with SST

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to a torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: BLEED BRAKE LINE

atten3.ptxt: Bleed the air from the brake line of the wheel farthest from the master cylinder.

s2.ptxt: Connect a vinyl tube to the bleeder plug.
Depress the brake pedal several times, then loosen the bleeder plug with the pedal depressed (*3).
When fluid stops coming out, tighten the bleeder plug, then release the brake pedal (*4).
Repeat (*3) and (*4) until all the air in the fluid is completely bled out.
Tighten the bleeder plug completely.
Repeat the above procedure for each wheel to bleed the air from the brake line.

s-1.ptxt: BLEED BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY (w/ VSC)

atten3.ptxt: After bleeding the air from the brake system, if the height or feel of the brake pedal cannot be obtained, bleed the air from the brake actuator assembly with the intelligent tester by following the procedures below.

s2.ptxt: Depress the brake pedal more than 20 times with the ignition switch off.
Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3, then turn the ignition switch on (IG).
Turn the intelligent tester on and select "AIR BLEEDING" on the screen.

item.ptxt: Refer to the intelligent tester operator's manual for farther details.
Bleed the air by following the steps displayed on the intelligent tester.

s2.ptxt: Bleed the air according to "Step 1: Increase" on the intelligent tester display.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the master cylinder reservoir tank does not become empty of brake fluid.

s3.ptxt: Connect a vinyl tube to either one of the bleeder plugs.
Depress the brake pedal several times, then loosen the bleeder plug connected to the vinyl tube with the pedal depressed (*5).
When fluid stops coming out, tighten the bleeder plug, then release the brake pedal (*6).
Repeat (*5) and (*6) until all the air in the fluid is completely bled out.
Tighten the bleeder plug completely.
Repeat the above procedures for the rest of the wheels to bleed the air from the brake line.

s2.ptxt: Bleed the air from the suction line according to "Step 2: Inhalation" on the intelligent tester display.

item.ptxt: Bleed the suction line by following the steps displayed on the intelligent tester.
Make sure that the master cylinder reservoir tank does not become empty of brake fluid.

s3.ptxt: Connect a vinyl tube to the bleeder plug at the right front wheel or the right rear wheel and loosen the bleeder plug.
Operate the brake actuator assembly to bleed the air using the intelligent tester (*7).

item.ptxt: The operation stops automatically in 4 seconds.
At this time, be sure to release the brake pedal.

s3.ptxt: Check that the operation has stopped by referring to the intelligent tester display and tighten the bleeder plug (*8).
Repeat (*7) and (*8) until all the air in the fluid is completely bled out.
Tighten the bleeder plug completely.
For the rest of the wheels, bleed the air in the same way as stated in the above procedures.

s2.ptxt: Bleed the air from the pressure reduction line according to "Step 3: Decrease" on the intelligent tester display.

item.ptxt: Bleed the pressure reduction line by following the steps displayed on the intelligent tester.
Make sure that the master cylinder reservoir tank does not become empty of brake fluid.

s3.ptxt: Connect a vinyl tube to either one of the bleeder plugs.
Loosen the bleeder plug (*9).
Using the intelligent tester, operate the brake actuator assembly, completely depress the brake pedal, and hold it.

item.ptxt: The operation stops automatically in 4 seconds. When performing this procedure continuously, an interval of at least 20 seconds is required.
When the operation is completed, the brake pedal slightly goes down. This is a normal phenomenon when the solenoid opens.
During this procedure, the pedal seems heavy, but completely depress it so that the brake fluid comes out from the bleeder plug.
Be sure to keep the brake pedal depressed. Never depress and release the pedal repeatedly.

s3.ptxt: Tighten the bleeder plug, then release the brake pedal (*10).
Repeat steps (*9) to (*10) until all the air in the fluid is completely bled out.
Tighten the bleeder plug completely.
Repeat the above procedures for the rest of the brakes to bleed the air from the brake line.

s2.ptxt: Bleed the air from the brake line again according to "Step 4: Increase" on the intelligent tester display.

item.ptxt: Bleed the air by following the steps displayed on the intelligent tester.
Make sure that the master cylinder reservoir tank does not become empty of brake fluid.

s3.ptxt: Connect a vinyl tube to either one of the bleeder plugs.
Depress the brake pedal several times, then loosen the bleeder plug connected to the vinyl tube with the pedal depressed (*11).
When fluid stops coming out, tighten the bleeder plug, then release the brake pedal (*12).
Repeat (*11) and (*12) until all the air in the fluid is completely bled out.
Tighten the bleeder plug completely.
Repeat the above procedures for each brake to bleed the air from the brake line.

s2.ptxt: Finish "AIR BLEEDING" on the intelligent tester and turn off the tester.
Disconnect the intelligent tester from the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch off.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT FLUID LEVEL IN RESERVOIR

s2.ptxt: Check the fluid level and add fluid if necessary.

specitem.ptxt: SAE J1703 or FMVSS No. 116 DOT3

s2.ptxt: If fluid leaks, tighten or replace the leaking part.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT FOR BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE
INSTALL COWL TOP OUTER FRONT PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the cowl top outer front panel sub-assembly with the 4 nuts, the 4 bolts and engage the clamp.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL WINDSHIELD WIPER MOTOR AND LINK ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the windshield wiper motor and link assembly with the 4 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Tighten the bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL TOP VENTILATOR LOUVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 claws and install the cowl top ventilator louver sub-assembly.
Install the 2 clips.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL LH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal LH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL RH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal RH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly LH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly RH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s2.ptxt: Operate the front wipers while spraying washer fluid on the windshield glass. Make sure that the front wipers function properly and the wipers do not come into contact with the vehicle body.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL AIR CLEANER FILTER ELEMENT SUB-ASSEMBLY
INSTALL AIR CLEANER CAP SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the air cleaner filter element onto the air cleaner case.
Insert the hinges. Install the air cleaner cap sub-assembly with the 2 bolts.
Align the matchmarks of the No. 1 air cleaner hose and throttle body, and then connect the air cleaner hose No. 1 to the throttle body and unfasten the No. 1 air cleaner hose clamp.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the hose clamp is at the correct angle.

s2.ptxt: Connect the No. 2 ventilation hose to the air cleaner hose.
Connect the purge line hose to the clamp (*1).
Connect the 2 purge VSV vacuum hoses (*2).
Connect the purge VSV connector (*3).
Connect the mass air flow meter connector (*4).
